Oracc: HBTIN — Hellenistic Babylonia: Texts, Iconography, Names

VS 15, 21 [undetermined][via hbtin]

Obverse
o 1o 1

[... m]⸢se⸣-lu-ku LUGAL mdna-na-a-MU mDU-[A ...]

(o 1) [month. day. year] Seleucus (was) king. Nanaya-iddin, Mukin-[apli ...] x-ahhe-iddin, Anu-balassu-iqbi and Labaši [...], their brother, sons of [...] voluntarily [...] are theirs in perpetuity.

o 22

[...]-⸢ŠEŠ⸣-MEŠ-MU md60-DIN-su-E u₃ mNU-[TEŠ₂]

o 33

[...] ŠEŠ-šu₂-nu A-MEŠ ša₂ ⸢m⸣[...] ⸢x⸣

o 44

[... ina ḫu]-⸢ud lib₃⸣-bi-šu₂ [...] ⸢x⸣

o 55

[...] ša₂

o 66

[...] mar

o 77

[...] ⸢x⸣

o 88

[...]

o 99

[...] ⸢x⸣ [...]

(9 lines missing)
o 1919

[...] ⸢x⸣

o 2020

[...] ṣa-a-tu₂ šu₂-nu

Reverse
r 1'r 1'

[...]

(r 1') [...] Anu-ša-x [...] ... Ina-qibit-Anu, his brother, the [...-man ...] x 11 shekels of silver, he took (and) to the property of Anu, he will give (it).

r 2'2'

[...] ⸢x x⸣ d60 ⸢ša₂⸣ [...]

r 3'3'

[...] ⸢i x⸣ [x x] ⸢m⸣ina-⸢qi₂-bit⸣-d60 ŠEŠ-šu₂ lu₂⸢x⸣-[...]

r 4'4'

[...] ⸢x 11⸣ GIN₂ KU₃.BABBAR il-qe₂ ⸢x⸣ a-na NIG₂.GA d60 i-nam-[din ...]

uninscribed line
(witnesses)
r 5'5'

lu₂mu-kin₇ md60-DINiṭ A ⸢ša₂⸣ m⸢d60⸣-ŠEŠ-GAL₂ši A mE₂.KUR-za-⸢kir⸣

(r 5') Witnesses: Anu-uballiṭ, son of Anu-ah-ušabši, descendant of Ekur-zakir

r 6'6'

md60-ik-ṣur A ša₂ m⸢BARA₂-d60⸣ A mkur-⸢i⸣

(r 6') Anu-ikṣur, son of Kidin-Anu, descendant of Kuri

r 7'7'

md60-AD-URI₃ A ša₂ mMUa A mŠEŠ-ʾu-u₂-⸢tu⸣

(r 7') Anu-ab-uṣur, son of Iddinaya, descendant of Ahʾutu

r 8'8'

md60-ŠEŠ-MU A ša₂ mri-ḫat-dINANA A mE₂.KUR-za-⸢kir⸣

(r 8') Anu-ah-iddin, son of Rihat-Ištar, descendant of Ekur-zakir

r 9'9'

md60-NUMUN-MU A ša₂ mla-ba-ši A mE₂.KUR-za-[kir]

(r 9') Anu-zer-iddin, son of Labaši, descendant of Ekur-zakir

r 10'10'

md60-NUMUN-GIŠ A ša₂ ⸢m⸣d⸢60⸣-ŠEŠ-MEŠ-MU A mḫun-⸢zu⸣-[u₂]

(r 10') Anu-zer-lišir, son of Anu-ahhe-iddin, descendant of Hunzu

r 11'11'

md60-AD-URI₃ A ša₂ mla-ba-ši A mE₂.KUR-[za-kir]

(r 11') Anu-ab-uṣur, son of Labaši, descendant of Ekur-zakir

r 12'12'

md60-DINiṭ A ša₂ mba-as-si-ia A mE₂.[KUR-za-kir]

(r 12') Anu-uballiṭ, son of Bassia, descendant of Ekur-zakir

(colophon)
2 lines uninscribed
r 13'13'

[m]⸢ni⸣-din-tu₄-d60 lu₂UMBISAG ⸢A⸣ [...]

(r 13') Nidintu-Anu, scribe, son of [...] [Uruk.] Abu. [x-th] day [year] Se[leušus ...]
